# Campaign Board

Use this reference when the task is about bingqi, warfare-lite design, command boards, front posture, route pressure, mobilization, or campaign transitions.

## Core Rule

For Zongzu, a campaign board is not a unit-micro wargame.
It is also not the only valid conflict surface.

It should read as:
- command pressure
- mobilization timing
- route and crossing control
- grain-line sustainment
- regional support and fear
- aftermath and political consequence

## Board Objects

- fronts
- approaches
- county or prefecture support regions
- grain routes
- depots and granaries
- ferries, bridges, crossings, and passes
- mustering pools
- garrison or escort detachments
- reserve markers

## State Tracks

- muster state
- command fit
- supply state
- route safety
- morale or discipline
- local support
- exhaustion
- aftermath pressure

## Good Player Directives

- accelerate muster
- hold reserve
- repair route
- secure crossing
- escort grain
- punish raiders
- avoid decisive battle
- consolidate and recover
- press pursuit
- trade prestige for sustainment

## Good Transitions

- stable to threatened route
- threatened to broken grain line
- idle to mustering force
- contained front to spreading front
- victory to overextension
- suppression to retaliation wave
- campaign end to blame, merit, and household burden

## Anti-Patterns

- tactical flanking micro as the dominant loop
- one counter per tiny unit
- modern command dashboard language
- generic fantasy battle UI that could belong to any game
- ignoring ferries, rivers, grain, and administrative support
- using the campaign board for every family clash, escort fight, raid, arrest, or market incident that should stay at a smaller scale

## Suggested Module Mapping

- `ConflictAndForce`
- `WarfareCampaign`
- `WorldSettlements`
- `OfficeAndCareer`
- `Presentation.Unity`
